original_image_tags: A line graph titled "Titan IV Random-Attitude Failures through 300 sec" plotting "Percent in 5-deg sector (%)" on a logarithmic Y-axis from 0.1 to 100 versus "Angle From Flight Path (deg)" on a linear X-axis from 0 to 180. The chart displays multiple data series representing different "Breakup q-alpha in deg-lb/ft²" values, distinguished by markers: no breakup (inverted triangle), 20,000 (solid circle), 10,000 (open circle), 5,000 (open square). Overlaid are four line fit styles corresponding to different constants:

elements.description: Line graph plotting 'Percent in 5-deg sector (%)' (y-axis, log scale from 0.1 to 100) against 'Angle From Flight Path (deg)' (x-axis, 0 to 180). Title: 'Titan IV Random-Attitude Failures through 300 sec'. Legend includes breakup q-alpha conditions: no breakup, 20,000, 10,000, 5,000, and four lines representing fitting constants A and B (e.g., A = 2.70, B = 10,000). Caption: 'Figure 28. Titan Simulation Results with Best-Fit Shaping Constants'.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the graph's title, axis labels, scales, data series categories, and legend items, with no contradictions in quoted text or visual element counts.
